mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 2002 engine rebuild cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard M10 rebuild typically costs £4,000–£6,000 supply only [PATTERN DATA]. A tii rebuild costs £6,000–£10,000+; a Turbo rebuild costs £8,000–£12,000+. Fitting and specialist labour add significantly. Use our calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: What engine is in the BMW 2002?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 2002 is powered by the M10 — a 1,990 cc inline-four petrol engine. The standard 2002 produces 100 hp; the ti produces 120 hp; the tii produces 130 hp; the Turbo produces 170 hp.

The M10 is one of BMW's most durable engines — over 3.5 million were produced across many models. However, age-related issues (cooling system, timing chain, Kugelfischer injection) require specialist attention. A well-maintained M10 can last hundreds of thousands of miles.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my 2002 engine needs attention?

acceptedAnswer.text: By issue: rattling on startup (timing chain stretch), uneven running (Kugelfischer injection synchronisation on tii/Turbo), overheating (cooling system deterioration), water in oil (cylinder head cracking). On the M10, any running issue is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a service; ignored, it's a £4,000–£12,000+ rebuild.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth rebuilding a BMW 2002 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the model. Against a £25,000–£40,000 tii, a £6,000–£10,000+ rebuild is 15–40% — ✅ a worthy investment. Against a £60,000–£80,000+ Turbo, a £8,000–£12,000+ rebuild is 10–20% — ✅ a worthy investment. Against a £10,000–£15,000 standard 2002, a £4,000–£6,000 rebuild is 27–60% — ⚠️ watch on cheaper cars.

2002tii with the M15 — the sweet spot: 130 hp, Kugelfischer mechanical fuel injection, and the most desirable non-Turbo 2002. For the ultimate collectible, 2002 Turbo — Europe's first turbocharged production car, only 1,672 built. For the accessible entry point, standard 2002 — £10,000–£15,000 for a driver-quality car.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 2002 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; the block stamping is visible from the driver's side. The 2002 uses the M05, M10, M15, M31 or M115 engine codes depending on the model.

mainEntity.name: BMW 2002 vs 3.0 CSL — which is more collectible?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.0 CSL is the more collectible of the two — only 1,265 CSLs were built, compared to 401,947 2002s. The CSL is a six-figure classic; the 2002 is a rising classic that is more accessible. Both are BMW icons and worthy investments.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 2002 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW 2002 — verified codes, specifications and real-world restoration insights.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: M10 family rating: Expert-knowledge tier — the M10 is one of BMW's most documented engines, with over 3.5 million produced across many models. However, no proprietary enquiry data exists for the 2002 due to its classic status. All figures are based on specialist workshops, BMW Group Classic, and enthusiast records. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The 2002's M10 engine produced 100 hp in standard form, not 98 hp as some sources state. Verified against BMW specifications. Correction applied: The 2002tii produces 130 hp — not 125 hp or 140 hp as some sources state. Factory rating is 130 hp at 5,800 rpm. Correction applied: The 2002 Turbo produces 170 hp — not 167 hp. BMW's official figure is 170 hp (125 kW). Correction applied: The 2002ti uses the M05 engine code with twin Solex side-draft carburettors — not a separate engine code. Verified against BMW parts catalogues. Correction applied: The 2002 was produced from 1968 to 1976, with the 1502 continuing until 1977. Total production was 401,947 units.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common restoration considerations for the M10 engine — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The M10's timing chain can stretch over time, causing rattling and potential valve timing issues. Affects all M10 engines. £500–£1,500 replacement.
Older cylinder heads have been known to crack and warp — water in the oil and a sweet smell around the engine are tell-tale signs. Affects high-mileage M10 engines. £1,000–£3,000 repair.

The mechanical fuel injection system on the tii and Turbo requires specialist servicing — age-related seal degradation and pump wear are common. Affects M15 and M31 engines. £2,000–£5,000 restoration.
Plastic and rubber in the cooling system gets brittle with age — run away at any sign of overheating. Affects all 2002s. £500–£1,500 restoration.
Age-related gasket deterioration causes oil leaks. Affects all M10 engines. £200–£800 repair.
The KKK turbocharger and Kugelfischer injection on the Turbo are complex and expensive to restore — only 1,672 built. Specialist-only work.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW 2002 Market Intelligence — What Collectors Told Us

signals.signal: ⬆ Rising — the tii is the most desirable non-Turbo

insights: Interesting insight: The 2002 is the car that saved BMW — it established the brand's reputation for sporty, driver-focused saloons and paved the way for the 3 Series. With 401,947 units produced, it's not rare, but well-preserved examples are becoming increasingly valuable.

The 2002tii is the sweet spot — 130 hp, Kugelfischer mechanical fuel injection, and the most desirable non-Turbo 2002. A well-restored tii can command £30,000–£40,000, while project cars start at £5,000–£10,000.

The 2002 Turbo is the ultimate collectible — Europe's first turbocharged production car, only 1,672 built, all left-hand drive. Values have risen steadily, with the best examples exceeding £60,000–£80,000+.

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW 2002 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one 2002 based on this page, buy the best-documented 2002tii you can afford — the Kugelfischer-injected M15 is the sweet spot: 130 hp, mechanical fuel injection, and the most desirable non-Turbo 2002. If the Turbo is calling you, budget for specialist KKK turbo and Kugelfischer restoration — only 1,672 were built, and they're only going one way. On any 2002, a documented M10 rebuild with timing chain and cooling system work is worth more than any service stamp.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW 2002 Engine Rebuild & Restoration Costs — By Model
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for M10 engine work — concours rebuilds, specialist servicing, and restoration economics. Supply-only figures for a rising classic.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: All figures [PATTERN DATA] — based on specialist workshops, BMW Group Classic, and auction records.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: M10 engine work is specialist-only — expect 35–80 hours at £65–£100/hr for classic BMW specialists [PATTERN DATA]. Kugelfischer injection and Turbo work require additional specialist time. The 2002 rule — restoration, not replacement: A standard M10 rebuild at £4,000–£6,000 against a £10,000–£15,000 car is 27–60% — ⚠️ watch on cheaper cars. A tii rebuild at £6,000–£10,000+ against a £25,000–£40,000 car is 15–40% — ✅ a worthy investment. A Turbo rebuild at £8,000–£12,000+ against a £60,000–£80,000+ car is 10–20% — ✅ a worthy investment. The 2002 is a rising classic — the tii and Turbo justify the investment; the standard 2002 is the accessible entry point.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW 2002 Engine Range Evolved — 1968 to 1976

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the 2002 as the sporty compact saloon — the M10 engine was a 2.0L evolution of the earlier 1.8L M10 in the 1800.
eras.worthKnowing: The 2002 saved BMW from financial ruin — it established the brand's reputation for sporty, driver-focused saloons.

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW added twin Solex side-draft carburettors to the M10 for the 2002ti — more power, more responsiveness.
eras.worthKnowing: The ti is rare — only a few thousand were built. It's the direct predecessor to the tii.

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW introduced Kugelfischer mechanical fuel injection for the 2002tii — 130 hp, better response, and improved emissions.
eras.worthKnowing: The tii is the sweet spot — Kugelfischer injection, 130 hp, and the most desirable non-Turbo 2002.

eras.era: The Turbo — First of Its Kind
eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW created the 2002 Turbo — Europe's first turbocharged production car. The KKK turbocharger and Kugelfischer injection produced 170 hp.
eras.worthKnowing: Only 1,672 Turbos were built, all left-hand drive. It's the ultimate 2002 collectible.

profiles.verdict: The 2002 is a rising classic — the tii is the sweet spot, the Turbo is the ultimate collectible. Both are appreciating assets. Buy the best-documented example you can afford.
Values have risen steadily — the best Turbos now exceed £80,000, and tii values are climbing. The 2002 is a preservation asset, not a depreciating car.

The 2002 is a driver's car — the M10 engine is robust, the chassis is communicative, and the driving experience is pure BMW. The tii is the sweet spot; the standard 2002 is the accessible entry point.
Project cars start at £5,000–£10,000 — but expect a £10,000–£20,000+ restoration bill. The M10 engine is rebuildable, but rust is the real enemy. Buy the best shell you can afford.
The standard 2002 is the accessible entry point — £10,000–£15,000 for a driver-quality car, £4,000–£6,000 for a M10 rebuild. The 2002 is a manageable classic for a first-time buyer.

signals.title: Built on Specialist Knowledge
signals.text: the 2002 is a classic-era vehicle with no proprietary enquiry data available. Our expertise comes from BMW Group Classic, specialist workshops, and enthusiast records.

signals.title: We Tell You When to Invest
signals.text: On a tii or Turbo, the answer is always restore. On a standard 2002, we'll run the numbers honestly — if the rebuild cost exceeds 70% of your car's value, we'll say so before you spend.
